title: muscaria atropurpurea
intro: Muscaria atropurpurea, also known as the Atropine plant, is a species of plant in the nightshade family that contains toxic alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Muscaria atropurpurea are derived from its tropane alkaloids, specifically atropine, which have anticholinergic effects and are used to treat various conditions. The health benefits of Muscaria atropurpurea include its ability to relieve muscle spasms, reduce salivation and sweating, and dilate pupils, making it a useful treatment for conditions such as glaucoma and myasthenia gravis. Muscaria atropurpurea is typically prepared as a tincture or dried extract, and it should be used under the guidance of a medical professional due to its potential toxicity.
